project.title: Cryo-EM of cardiac AL-224L amyloid reveals shared features in l6 light chain fibril folds
project.projectDescription: Structural characterization of an amyloid fibril from a patient afected by cardiac light chain amyloidosis.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Fibrils were extracted from the tissue block as previously described (Swuec et al., Nature Commun 2019), using overnight tissue digestion with C. histolyticum collagenase (5 mg/ml in a buffer containing 20 mM Tris, 140 mM NaCl, 2 mM CaCl2, pH 8.0), followed by 15 cycles of homogenization in Tris EDTA buffer (20 mM Tris, 140 mM NaCl, 10 mM EDTA, pH 8.0) to remove soluble proteins, and by repeated homogenization of the remaining pellet in ultrapure water. The fibril-containing supernatants from the water homogenization cycles were retained. The cryo-EM and proteomic analyses were performed using the supernatant from the third water-extraction cycle (water fraction 3).For proteomic analysis of the extracted fibrils (water fraction 3), 25 μg of proteins were subjected to in-solution digestion with MS-grade trypsin (Thermo Fisher Scientific; enzyme:protein ratio 1:20) overnight at 37 °C. For excised protein spots #15 and #16 from the 2D SDS-PAGE, in-gel digestion was performed as described (Swuec et al., Nature Commun 2019).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Data were processed using the Proteome Discoverer software (Thermo Fisher Scientific) version 3.1, using a human protein database downloaded in November 2024 from UNIPROT and augmented with the AL-224L sequence. The following criteria were used for identification: cleavage enzyme trypsin (semi-specific); cysteines carbamidomethylation as static modification; methionine oxidation and conversion of N-terminal glutamine into pyroglutamate as dynamic modifications.

references.referenceLine: Hicks CW, Prokaeva T, Spencer B, Jayaraman S, Huda N, Wong S, Chen H, Sanchorawala V, Lavatelli F, Gursky O. Cryo-EM of Cardiac AL-224L Amyloid Reveals Shared Structural Motifs and Mutation-induced Differences in λ6 Light Chain Fibrils. J Mol Biol. 2026 438(3):169591
